response.justification: Candidate is an experienced full-stack engineer (~13+ years) with strong web, distributed systems, and DLang background, but the role specifically demands a senior C++ developer with deep STL/OOP, multi-threaded systems, MQTT, and IEC 62443 industrial cybersecurity experience. The only substantive C/C++ work on the resume is a 6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015, with no industrial automation, secure development lifecycle, or relevant protocol exposure (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850, etc.). The skill list does not even include C++, making this a poor fit for a senior C++ architect position.
strengths: 13+ years of software engineering experience across multiple domains
Strong distributed/event-driven systems background (low-latency micro-services at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby)
Demonstrated mentoring and technical communication via workshops, talks, and Selenium course
concerns: Minimal recent C++ experience — only ~6 months at National Instruments a decade ago
No exposure to IEC 62443, Secure Development Lifecycle, or industrial cybersecurity standards
No industrial automation, MQTT, or relevant industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850, DNP 3.0)

key_factors.factor: Strong general senior engineering and distributed systems experience
